snippets.title: Beta–Bernoulli posterior update
snippets.code:
from scipy.stats import beta

# Prior: Beta(alpha, beta_param)
alpha, beta_param = 2, 2  # slight bias toward 0.5

# Data: s successes in n trials
s, n = 8, 10

post_alpha = alpha + s
post_beta = beta_param + (n - s)

print(f"Prior mean: {alpha/(alpha+beta_param):.3f}")
print(f"Posterior mean: {post_alpha/(post_alpha+post_beta):.3f}")
print(f"Posterior 95% interval: {beta.ppf([0.025,0.975], post_alpha, post_beta)}")
snippets.explanation: Conjugate updating adds successes to α and failures to β. The posterior mean is a precision-weighted blend of prior mean and sample proportion.

snippets.title: Compare weak vs strong priors on the same data
snippets.code:
from scipy.stats import beta

s, n = 3, 4  # 3 heads in 4 flips

for name, a, b in [("weak Beta(1,1)", 1, 1), ("strong Beta(20,20)", 20, 20)]:
    pa, pb = a + s, b + (n - s)
    print(name, "mean=", pa/(pa+pb))
snippets.explanation: With sparse data, a strong prior near 0.5 barely moves; a weak prior tracks the sample proportion 0.75 closely.

snippets.title: Rare disease posterior (Bayes' rule)
snippets.code:
prior = 0.001
sens = 0.99
spec = 0.99

p_pos = sens * prior + (1 - spec) * (1 - prior)
posterior = sens * prior / p_pos
print(f"P(diseased|+) = {posterior:.4f}")
snippets.explanation: Explicit prior and likelihood pieces compute the normalization P(+) and posterior P(diseased|+) without memorizing a formula.
